ops.title: Runbook surface-layer implementation spec (CLI, MCP, viz, FUSE, docs)
ops.body:
# Runbook surface-layer implementation spec (Phases 2-4)

Approved-plan companion. Assumes the Phase 1 core exists per the core spec (Runbook/RunbookStep/RunbookRun with `Results []RunbookStepResult`, statuses done|failed|skipped, Comments IN, Commits OUT, `model.PositionBetween`, `refs.KindRunbook`, `store.ListRunbooks`, `fold.Runbook`, trail cases). Reconciled: no `run pending` verb (pending is absence — re-mark to correct), no `--link-commit`/`commit_linked` (no Commits field), no `run exec` in v1.

## Recon corrections (verified)
1. `registerPlanning` (internal/mcpserver/tools_planning.go:57-150) registers add/edit/list/show/comment/status-transitions — the "curated" precedent means "omit low-frequency verbs", not "add/edit/list only".
2. `web/src/browse/FilterBar.tsx` needs NO runbook edits — facets derive from KINDS/rows; `active`/`archived` already in `STATUS_RANK` (browse/index.ts:207-216).
3. record.py third touchpoint: `INTERNAL_BODY_RE` (record.py:54) contains `\brunbook\b`; plugin/hooks/README.md:65 names runbook. Neither needs removal — only the routing destination changes.
4. viz Go must land atomically with anything minting runbook refs: `entityRefs` (internal/viz/viz.go:242-256) auto-discovers via wildcard walk; `summaryOf` panics; `classify` degrades gracefully.
5. fusefs is partially tag-gated: paths.go and render.go are untagged pure Go (unit-testable everywhere); fs.go/attachments.go/fuse.go carry `//go:build fuse`.
6. `compact` CLI excludes sprints/projects by documented contract (compact.go:58-61) — runbook stays out too; but `ambiguousAcrossKinds` (compact.go:110-123) needs a `model.Runbook` title case because history/show share it.

## 1. CLI — `internal/cli/runbook.go` (new)

Command tree:
```
cc-notes runbook
├── add TITLE          --body|-  --label(rep)  --step TEXT(rep)  --json
├── list               --all  --json
├── show ID            --json
├── activate ID        --json     (factory: newRunbookStatusCmd)
├── archive ID         --json
├── edit ID            --title --body --add-label --rm-label --json
├── comment ID [BODY]  --json     (sprint comment pattern, sprint.go:318)
├── history ID         --json --reverse --limit
├── step
│   ├── add RUNBOOK TEXT     --command TEXT  --first|--last|--before STEP|--after STEP  --json
│   ├── rm RUNBOOK STEP      --json
│   ├── edit RUNBOOK STEP    --text TEXT  --command TEXT  --no-command  --json
│   ├── move RUNBOOK STEP    --first|--last|--before STEP|--after STEP  --json
│   └── list RUNBOOK         --json
└── run
    ├── start RUNBOOK        --task ID  --json
    ├── list RUNBOOK         --json
    ├── show RUNBOOK RUN     --json
    ├── done RUNBOOK STEP    --note TEXT  --run RUN  --json
    ├── skip RUNBOOK STEP    --note TEXT  --run RUN  --json
    ├── fail RUNBOOK STEP    --note TEXT  --run RUN  --json
    └── finish RUNBOOK       --run RUN  --failed|--abandoned  --json
```

Semantics:
- `add`: mirrors `newSprintAddCmd` (sprint.go:36): validateTitle, autoInstall, bodyArg; ops = CreateRunbook + one AddStep per `--step` (flag order, positions via successive `PositionBetween(last, "")`); warnDuplicate on dedup; echo via printRunbook.
- `list`: default active only; `--all` includes archived.
- `show`: MUST render steps + runs in plain text (do NOT copy renderTaskShow's criteria omission, output.go:673-701).
- `activate`/`archive`: factory like newSprintStatusCmd (sprint.go:176); already-in-state → &ConflictError{} (exit 4). Archived-runbook writes across all mutating verbs gated with ConflictError before Append (sprint.go:195-198 pattern).
- `edit`: SetTitle/SetDescription/AddLabel/RemoveLabel; "at least one flag" UsageError.
- `history`: one line — `kindHistoryCmd(refs.KindRunbook, "runbook")` (history.go:32-37 block).
- `step add`/`move` placement: `stepPlacement` struct binds --first/--last/--before/--after (exactly one; add defaults --last; move requires one; mutual exclusion → UsageError). CLI computes Position from the folded snapshot via `model.PositionBetween(prev, next)` — never reimplemented.
- step/run prefix resolution: `resolveStep(rb, prefix)` / `resolveRun(rb, prefix)` in common.go, byte-for-byte mirrors of resolveCriterion (common.go:325-348); run ambiguity candidates listed as `<id7> <status> <started date>`.
- Default-run resolution (--run omitted on done/skip/fail/finish): exactly one running run → use it; zero → ConflictError ("no running run for <short>; start one with `runbook run start` or pass --run"); several → ErrAmbiguous listing them. `--run` accepts a prefix and may target finished runs (record correction — core upserts post-finish).
- `run start`: --task resolves via loadTask, cites full EntityID. Concurrent runs allowed (no guard).
- `run finish`: --failed/--abandoned mutually exclusive; default status: any step result failed → failed, else succeeded. Op FinishRun{ID, Status}.
- didyoumean.go:80 subcommandHints: consider `"run": {"reset": 'a wrong mark is corrected by re-marking (runbook run done|skip|fail)'}`.

DTOs and rendering — internal/cli/output.go (next to sprintDTO :164), built with emptyNotNil/shaStrings/rfc3339/optTime/optString conventions of newSprintDTO (:482):

```go
type runbookStepDTO struct { ID, Text, Command, Position string }          // json tags id/text/command/position
type runbookRunStepDTO struct { Step, Status, Note string }                // note omitempty
type runbookRunDTO struct {
    ID string; Task *string; Runner string; Status string
    StartedAt string; FinishedAt *string
    Steps []runbookRunStepDTO   // rendered in runbook step order; always non-nil
}
type runbookDTO struct {
    ID, Title, Description, Status string
    Steps []runbookStepDTO; Runs []runbookRunDTO
    Labels []string; Author, CreatedAt, UpdatedAt string; ArchivedAt *string
}
```

Lean line (3-field sprint parity, cli-reference.md:51-58): `<short7>\t<status>\t<title>`.
`step list` lean line: `<short7-step>\t<n>\t<text>\t<command|->` (n = 1-based position index).
`run list` lean line: `<short7-run>\t<status>\t<runner>\t<YYYY-MM-DD>\t<done+skipped>/<total>`.

`renderRunbookShow`: header block (id/title/status/labels/created/updated/archived) → description → `steps:` numbered `[<step-short7>] <text>` with indented `$ <command>` when set → `runs:` newest-first, capped at 5 with `(+N older — use run list)`; run line: `-- <run-short7> <status> by <runner> <started> → <finished|running> (X done, Y skipped, Z failed / N) task <task-short7|->`.
`renderRunShow`: run header + one line per step in runbook order: `<step-short7> <status> <text>` + indented `note: …` when set.
common.go: `loadRunbook` (mirror loadSprint :403), `printRunbook` (mirror printSprint :623).

Global-resolution touches:
- history.go:87 entityKinds += refs.KindRunbook; :29 Short text += runbook.
- show.go:22 Short text; :39-54 switch += `case refs.KindRunbook: showRunbook(...)` (mirror showSprint :161).
- compact.go:110-123 ambiguousAcrossKinds += `case model.Runbook` (resolveEntity for compact stays note/task/doc/log per its documented contract).
- root.go:26 AddCommand(... newRunbookCmd() ...) after newProjectCmd(); package comment noun list.
- history.go formatTrailElement (:233-247): `case "steps": return fmt.Sprintf("%q", m["text"])`; `case "runs": return fmt.Sprintf("run by %s [%s]", scalarString(m["runner"]), scalarString(m["status"]))`.

## 2. MCP — `internal/mcpserver/tools_runbook.go` (new), register in server.go:35

9 tools (full run loop in — agents are the primary executors; definition micro-editing stays CLI-only):

| Tool | Args | CLI argv |
|---|---|---|
| runbook_add | title; body?; labels?[]; steps?[] | runbook add TITLE --json [--body][--label…][--step…] |
| runbook_list | all? | runbook list --json [--all] |
| runbook_show | id (reuse entityIDArgs) | runbook show ID --json |
| runbook_step_add | id; text; command? | runbook step add ID TEXT --json [--command] |
| runbook_run_start | id; task? | runbook run start ID --json [--task] |
| runbook_run_done | id; step; note?; run? | runbook run done ID STEP --json [--note][--run] |
| runbook_run_skip | id; step; note?; run? | (same shape) |
| runbook_run_fail | id; step; note?; run? | (same shape) |
| runbook_run_finish | id; run?; failed?; abandoned? | runbook run finish ID --json [--run][--failed][--abandoned] |

done/skip/fail generated by a verb loop like sprint transitions (tools_planning.go:105-110) — do NOT expose a status-enum param that selects the verb (TestMCPVocabularyMatchesCobra synthesizes argv and would hit "unknown command"). inventory_test.go:121 needs zero edits (dynamic) but must pass: every flag literal must exist on real commands; synthesized combos may only fail semantically.
instructions.go: append one run-loop clause ("A repeatable operational procedure → runbook_add; execute with runbook_run_start → runbook_run_done/skip/fail → runbook_run_finish."); keep under the 2KB truncation — trim an existing redundancy if the ~140-word guidance is exceeded.

## 3. viz

### Go
- events_classify.go: `entityRunbook = "runbook"` const (:10-17); classify (:52-65) += `case model.Runbook: return runbookEvents(entry)`; entityTitle (:197-214) += case; branchOf: NO case (trunk, like sprint/project).
- New event consts (:24-37 block): `evRunStarted = "run_started"`, `evRunFinished = "run_finished"`.
- runbookEvents: trailCreate → created; `changeFor(changes,"status")` → status; `changeFor(changes,"runs")`: added element m with m["status"]=="running" and no removed element sharing m["id"] → run_started (detail run short id + task when set); added element with terminal status → run_finished (detail run + status); added/removed pair sharing id, both running (a step-result update) → single edited; step/other changes → edited. Written against trail's set-of-objects diff (elements are maps carrying id/status — same shape criteria produce).
- events_summary.go entities() (:19-79) += ListRunbooks loop; new `runbookSummary(rb)` → EntitySummary{Kind: entityRunbook, ID, Short, Title, Status} (types.go:94 needs no new fields).
- api.go entityKind (:168-185) += `case entityRunbook: return refs.KindRunbook, true`; summaryOf (:189-206) += `case model.Runbook: return runbookSummary(v)` (kills the panic).
- state.go stateResponse (:12-19) += `Runbooks []model.Runbook \`json:"runbooks"\`` + handleEntities ListRunbooks call.
- Timeline: runbook is a MARKER kind (no date range, no band). Event stream: created ● → run_started ▲ → edited markers → run_finished ■ → status ◆ on archive.

### Web (web/src/)
- api.ts: RunbookStepSnapshot/RunbookRunSnapshot/RunbookSnapshot interfaces matching core JSON tags exactly (results is an ORDERED LIST of {step_id,status,note,actor,ts}); Snapshot union (:325-331); StateResponse (:346-353) += runbooks; RawStateResponse + normalizeEntities (:468-477) += `runbooks: raw.runbooks ?? []`.
- route.ts:14 KINDS += "runbook".
- browse/index.ts: EntityKind union (:17); KINDS (:20) → ["task","note","doc","log","runbook","sprint","project"]; new runbookRow(s) (status = s.status; tags = labels; bodyLower = description + step texts + step commands + labels + id); buildIndex (:189-198) += spread after logs. statusRank unchanged.
- timeline/layout.ts:10 MARKER_KINDS += "runbook" (BAND_KINDS :12 unchanged); timeline/filters.ts:11 duplicated MARKER_KINDS += "runbook".
- timeline/Legend.tsx:12 KIND_ORDER += "runbook" (after "log").
- timeline/marks.ts:42-56 EVENT_SPECS += run_started {shape triangle-up, label "Run started"} and run_finished {shape square, label "Run finished"} — stay within the existing S-token color vocabulary; s4 is the free token.
- theme.css (~:1893 region): `.kind-runbook { background: color-mix(in srgb, var(--viz-s4) 20%, var(--bg)); }` (kind chips are pure CSS classes `kind-badge kind-${k}`, Legend.tsx:55).
- detail/Snapshot.tsx: SnapshotView switch (:23-37) += runbook case; RunbookView (pattern SprintView :245): Description (Markdown) → Steps (<ol>, step text + <code> command when set) → Runs (StatusBadge, runner, TimeText started→finished, per-step result lines with status + note, IdChip for task) → Labels chips.
- App.tsx:142 bucket spread += d.runbooks.
- FilterBar.tsx: NO change.

### viz tests
- golden_test.go buildGoldenEntities (:131-173): `createRunbook` helper (pattern createSprint :196) — create with two steps, StartRun, one SetRunStepStatus done, FinishRun succeeded — pinning created/edited/run_started/run_finished in graph_full.json + legend + entities bucket. Regenerate: `go test ./internal/viz -run Golden -update`. state_test.go (runbooks bucket) and api_test.go (/api/entity/runbook/<id>) one case each.
- events_test.go: runbookEvents table — create, status, run start, step-update-folds-to-edited, run finish (each terminal status).
- web vitest: **api.test.ts:175-205 MUST be edited** (null-bucket JSON literal + per-bucket assertions enumerate all kinds: add "runbooks":null / expect []). Plus runbookRow test, layout.test.ts runbook marker case (asserts band ABSENCE + marker presence, pattern :365), filters.test.ts presentKinds with a runbook event (:79-122).

## 4. Read-only FUSE

Precedent: the /attachments subtree is fully read-only today — underAttachments (attachments.go:33-36) gates Create → -EPERM (fs.go:213), Truncate → -EACCES (:276), Rename dest → -EPERM (:371); openAttachment rejects write-intent flags with -EACCES (attachments.go:127); Write on such handles → -EACCES (fs.go:259-261); stat mode 0o444 (attachments.go:232-234); Unlink already -EPERM for entities (fs.go:419).

**Errno: EACCES, not EROFS** — consistency with the proven attachments path through FUSE-T's NFS translation (which has bitten this code before; fs.go:304-309). Editor UX: 0o444 stat → vim opens [readonly]; :w! → EACCES "E212"; atomic-save editors die at Create/Rename with EPERM. No partial state, no scratch shadowing.

**Format: markdown** (read-only file has no parse→diff→ops round-trip constraint; procedure prose reads better than JSON). RenderRunbook: YAML frontmatter (id/title/status/labels/created/updated) + description + `## Steps` (numbered; step-id HTML comments `<!-- cc-notes:step <id7> -->` per the log-entry fence idiom, render.go:702-712; fenced ```sh blocks for commands) + `## Runs` summary lines (`- <id7> <status> — <runner>, <started> → <finished>, X done / Y skipped / Z failed (task <id7>)`).

File-by-file:
- paths.go: `RunbooksDir{}`, `RunbookFile{ShortID}` node types + node() markers (:179-204 block); `RunbookFilename(rb)` → `<short7>-<slug>.md` (NoteFilename pattern :208); ParsePath `case "runbooks"` — flat, .md-suffixed, "runbooks do not nest" (clone of "notes" case :308-320).
- render.go: `RenderRunbook(model.Runbook) []byte` ONLY. NO ParseRunbook/DiffRunbook/NewRunbook; diffDocument (:595) and newEntityOps (:726) get NO runbook case (panics stay as tripwires, unreachable because entityTarget (:774) gets NO /runbooks case and Open refuses write intent).
- fs.go:
  - `underRunbooks(p)` helper mirroring underAttachments.
  - Create (:209): underRunbooks → -EPERM (also prevents scratch shadowing).
  - Open (:175): after scratch check, underRunbooks && flags&(O_WRONLY|O_RDWR|O_TRUNC|O_APPEND) != 0 → -EACCES; else normal read handle via openEntity.
  - Write (:252): underRunbooks(h.path) → -EACCES (belt-and-braces: FUSE-T path-based handleFor fallback after NFS reconnects).
  - Truncate (:272): underRunbooks → -EACCES. Rename (:367): underRunbooks(newpath) → -EPERM at :371. Unlink: no change.
  - Getattr (:158): handle branch — underRunbooks(h.path) → S_IFREG|0o444.
  - resolveEntity (:707) += `case refs.KindRunbook: f.resolveRunbook(shortID)`; resolveRunbook mirrors resolveSprint (:959) over refs.RunbooksRoot.
  - openEntity (:816) += case RunbookFile; refFor (:793) += `case model.Runbook: return refs.Runbook(s.ID)` (totality).
  - renderDocument (:1046) += RenderRunbook case; headOf (:1065) + snapshotTimes (:1084) += model.Runbook cases (required — renderTip caches, Open stamps times).
  - statPath (:1106): RunbooksDir joins dir-stat (:1129); RunbookFile resolves then fills 0o444 stat — add `fillReadonlyEntityStat(stat, r)` beside fillEntityStat (:1577), identical except mode.
  - listDir (:1210): Root names (:1218) += "runbooks"; `case RunbooksDir:` lists store.ListRunbooks → RunbookFilename each — ALL runbooks incl. archived (a filesystem shouldn't hide resolvable entities; the file shows status).
  - **notesSeed (:1640): add the RunbookFile case** — without it FUSE-T's NFS client serves a stale render after a same-second external CLI commit (live-smoke bug documented at :1629-1639).

## 5. Docs / plugin

PROCESS: implementers MUST read the installed writing-docs skill (~/.claude/plugins/cache/*/skills/writing-docs/ or ~/Code/cc-skills/plugins/writing-docs/skills/writing-docs/ — SKILL.md + references) before writing prose, and run `slop-cop check <file> --lang=markdown` on every edited doc. This section specs WHAT changes where; the skill governs HOW.

- README.md (~:124): extend the optional-planning-layer sentence with runbook alongside sprint/project (reusable procedures whose runs are tracked per-execution). No new commands-table row. Stewardship: soften the :128 "MCP mirrors the CLI surface one-to-one" claim (already inexact for planning nouns).
- plugin/skills/using-cc-notes/SKILL.md: runbook does NOT join the five-tools table (:39-45). Extend the optional-layer prose (:334-345 "Projects and sprints (optional)") or add a sibling paragraph: what a runbook is, doc-vs-runbook (a doc describes; a runbook is executed, step-status per run), the run loop verbs, pointer to the new reference. Frontmatter description gains a trigger phrase. Command cheat-sheet gains runbook add / step add / run start / run done / run finish lines.
- references/cli-reference.md: intro noun enumeration (:3) + --json sentence (:4) += runbook; "The model in one screen" (:12-41) bullet (repo-wide like sprints/projects; step/run nonce ids prefix-resolve like criterion ids per :39); Lean-line table (:51-58) Runbook row + trailing note for step/run list line shapes (criterion-note pattern :60-62); new `## Runbook commands` section between Sprint commands (:848-966) and Note commands, one ### per verb in the sprint house style (flag table + console example), ending with `### JSON runbook shape`.
- NEW plugin/skills/using-cc-notes/references/runbooks.md: doc-vs-runbook decision + authoring/executing how-to (sibling of sprints-and-projects.md).
- plugin/hooks/record.py collision fix (minimal, no RECORD_KINDS wiring):
  1. RECORD_ROUTER_SYSTEM (:186-188): remove "a runbook" from the doc bullet's examples; add kind bullet: `- runbook: a repeatable step-by-step operational procedure meant to be re-executed (deploy steps, an incident-response checklist) — cc-notes has a first-class runbook primitive for these.` (RecordVerdict.kind is a free string — schema unchanged.)
  2. nudge_record_durable (:236-245): BEFORE the `verdict.kind not in RECORD_KINDS` early-return (which would silently swallow "runbook"), special-case kind == "runbook" → record_fire + evt.warn with a hand-written two-line suggestion (`cc-notes runbook add "<title>" --body -` then `cc-notes runbook step add <id> "<step>"`; MCP names runbook_add/runbook_step_add when mcp_active(evt)), bypassing record_command (common.py:174). RECORD_KINDS (common.py:23) stays ("note","doc","log","task").
  3. KEEP WEAK_INTERNAL_GLOBS runbook* (:43) and INTERNAL_BODY_RE \brunbook\b (:54) — detection is right; only routing destination changes. State this in the commit message.
  4. plugin/hooks/README.md:65: update the "LLM picks" column.
- Hook tests: inline `tests={...}` in the @on decorator stay Allow() (call_llm stub returns record=False). In plugin/hooks/tests/test_cc_notes.py: keep the :350 WEAK runbook* gate case; ADD a routed-verdict test with a record=True, kind="runbook" LLM stub asserting the warn names `cc-notes runbook` and NOT `doc add`. Run via `uv run pytest plugin/hooks/tests/test_cc_notes.py` (relative-import pack).

## 6. Test plan per surface

| Surface | Files | Cases |
|---|---|---|
| CLI | internal/cli/runbook_test.go (clone sprint_test harness) | add w/ --step order; list active vs --all; plain show contains steps+runs (gotcha regression); placement flags + move; step/run prefix ambiguity (mirror TestResolveCriterion common_test.go:11); run start --task; done/skip/fail default-run resolution (zero → conflict, two → ambiguous); finish default/--failed/--abandoned + mutual exclusion; archive/activate conflict (exit 4); comment; history; --json shapes |
| CLI internal | runbook_output_internal_test.go (mirror doc_output_internal_test.go) | leanRunbookLine, newRunbookDTO (non-nil slices, optTime), renderRunbookShow exact text |
| CLI global | didyoumean_test.go TestGlobalShow (:85) | runbook case in cross-kind show/history |
| MCP | none new | TestMCPVocabularyMatchesCobra passes |
| viz Go | golden (with -update), events_test.go, state_test.go, api_test.go | per §3 |
| web | api.test.ts (:175-205 edit), browse index, layout.test.ts, filters.test.ts | per §3 |
| fusefs untagged | render_test.go, paths_test.go | RenderRunbook shape; ParsePath /runbooks; filename slugging |
| fusefs fuse tag | fs_test.go | readdir lists file; read returns render; Open(O_RDWR)/Write/Truncate → EACCES; Create/Rename → EPERM; Getattr 0o444; external CLI append visible next read (notesSeed) |
ops.when: before implementing runbook CLI/MCP/viz/fusefs surfaces or the plugin record-router fix
